scenario: Next.js SSR render path tracing and request ID threading

steps.step: Orient in the Next.js server architecture

map_quality.rationale: Names Server (base-server.ts:316), NextNodeServer (next-server.ts:175), RouteModule, AppPageRouteModule, PagesRouteModule, ResponseCache, and the major subdirs (app-render/, route-modules/, response-cache/, async-storage/, web/) with file:line. The Pages-vs-App dispatch point at next-server.ts:616 plus concrete render.tsx vs app-render.tsx paths gives the agent a direct map to extend; NextCustomServer is not explicitly named, a minor gap.

specificity.rationale: Exact class names (NextNodeServer, AppPageRouteModule, RouteModule), file:line pairs throughout, and concrete file split (render.tsx:457/1622 vs app-render/app-render.tsx:3077/2568). Re-export sites and method line numbers are precise.

justification.rationale: Explains the role of each class ("Base class. Owns handleRequest...", "Per-route bundle interface", "Wraps incremental cache + in-memory LRU") and what each subdirectory is responsible for. Light on the Edge-vs-Node rationale for the class hierarchy specifically — web-server is named but its motivation isn't drawn out.

uncertainty.rationale: Mentions the edge runtime (web-server.ts) and the static-export path as separate code paths, and notes the App-vs-Pages dispatch branch — useful flags. But no explicit call-out of dev-vs-prod server split, Turbopack vs webpack, or minimal-mode/standalone variants the agent should double-check.

map_quality.rationale: Walks the full SSR pipeline with file:line at every layer: handleRequest (base-server.ts:877), handleRequestImpl (:968), run/pipe/render, renderToResponseImpl (:2579), renderToResponseWithComponents (:1974/2026), renderHTMLImpl (next-server.ts:616), and both renderers (app-render.tsx:3077, render.tsx:1622/457). An agent could insert a render-time hook at the right layer immediately.

specificity.rationale: Exported wrappers and inner Impls are explicitly distinguished (renderToHTMLOrFlight :3077 vs renderToHTMLOrFlightImpl :2568; renderToHTML :1622 vs renderToHTMLImpl :457), the outermost handleRequest entry is named, and lazyRenderAppPage/lazyRenderPagesPage shims are cited.

justification.rationale: Explains why the pipeline splits at renderHTMLImpl (enabledDirectories.app && renderOpts.isAppPath), what findPageComponents/loadComponents do at the boundary, and why pipeImpl is 'the single place response body is flushed'. Could go slightly deeper on the cache/render boundary rationale.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags RSC vs full HTML render branching in renderToHTMLOrFlightImpl and the response-cache interaction, plus the edge web-server parity. However, it does not explicitly call out streaming/Flight nuances, dynamic vs static prerender divergence, or response-cache bypass conditions that may divert the linear pipeline.

map_quality.rationale: Enumerates every caller with file:line, enclosing function, and subdir tag — module.ts:160 (AppPageRouteModule.render), module.render.ts:7-11 (lazyRenderAppPage), next-server.ts:635 (renderHTMLImpl), export/routes/app-page.ts:78 (exportAppPage), plus the self-reference at app-render.tsx:3158. Agent could change the signature confident sites are enumerated.

specificity.rationale: Callers cited with file:line + enclosing function; export sites named concretely (module.ts:9 import / :210 export, module.compiled.js:1-33 dispatcher). Even the AppPageRender type alias at :3066 and the Impl arg list at :2568 are pinned.

justification.rationale: Distinguishes each caller's role — request handler vs route module method vs lazy shim vs build-time export — and explains the module.compiled.js dispatch and the field-order constraint at :6849. Slightly under-explains why route-modules re-export.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags the runtime dispatch via module.compiled.js and the rebuild step for next-server/app-page.runtime bundles, and notes lazyRenderAppPage uses rest params so callers must be updated. Could have been more explicit about edge-runtime variant absence.

steps.step: Assess where to thread a request ID

map_quality.rationale: Names RequestMeta with file:line (request-meta.ts:51+, addRequestMeta), handleRequestImpl generation point (base-server.ts:968-979), ResponseCache interaction sites (response-cache/index.ts:107, route-module.ts:135), and concrete test directories. The agent has everything needed to open a PR without re-exploring.

specificity.rationale: Cites concrete addRequestMeta call, RequestMeta interface location, ResponseCacheEntry/CachedFetchValue.headers, and specific early-return lines (:2120, :2731, :2736). Slight ding: uses 'x-request-id' literal but doesn't locate a NEXT_REQUEST_ID_HEADER constant — though it does say to add the requestId key explicitly.

justification.rationale: Explicitly compares RequestMeta vs AsyncLocalStorage with three reasons ((a) survives Pages/App split, (b) precedent with initURL/cacheBustingSearchParam, (c) RequestStore only on App path), and explains why response-cache must not absorb the header (cache hits would replay original id).

uncertainty.rationale: Flags response-cache key/header inclusion explicitly, edge runtime via web-server.ts, middleware propagation, static export build-time stubs, and RSC cache-busting redirect branch. Covers all the interaction points the rubric calls for.
